title: Sądowa kontrola postępowania zmierzającego do wyłonienia kandydata na wolne stanowisko w służbie cywilnej
author: Kosicki, Tomasz
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: Recruitment regulated by the provisions of the Act of 21 November 2008 on the Civil Service, understood as the procedure aimed at selecting a candidate for a vacant position in the Civil Service, is highly formalized. In this respect, the legislator imposed a number of legal obligations on the recruitment organizers, which concern, among other things, the recruitment announcement, preparation of the recruitment protocol and the tasks of the competition committee. Its main task is to select the best candidate who meets the necessary requirements and best meets the additional requirements set out in the advertisement, which is an emanation of the principle of competitive recruitment. Competition proceedings in the civil service should be classified as proceedings in the field of public administration, which results in their being brought under the jurisdiction of administrative courts pursuant to Art. 3 § 2 point 4 of the Act of 30 August 2022 – Law on proceedings before administrative courts as other acts or activities in the field of public administration regarding rights or obligations arising from legal provisions. Therefore, the main goal of this study is to synthetically characterize the competition procedure and then to present the normative and axiological grounds for subjecting it to judicial and administrative control.
steps_raw: Recruitment regulated by the provisions of the Act of 21 November 2008 on the Civil Service, understood as the procedure aimed at selecting a candidate for a vacant position in the Civil Service, is highly formalized. In this respect, the legislator imposed a number of legal obligations on the recruitment organizers, which concern, among other things, the recruitment announcement, preparation of the recruitment protocol and the tasks of the competition committee. Its main task is to select the best candidate who meets the necessary requirements and best meets the additional requirements set out in the advertisement, which is an emanation of the principle of competitive recruitment. Competition proceedings in the civil service should be classified as proceedings in the field of public administration, which results in their being brought under the jurisdiction of administrative courts pursuant to Art. 3 § 2 point 4 of the Act of 30 August 2022 – Law on proceedings before administrative courts as other acts or activities in the field of public administration regarding rights or obligations arising from legal provisions. Therefore, the main goal of this study is to synthetically characterize the competition procedure and then to present the normative and axiological grounds for subjecting it to judicial and administrative control.
